ACL.summary: A container for Access List Control commands.

APPEND.summary: Appends a string to the value of a key. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

ASKING.summary: Signals that a cluster client is following an -ASK redirect.

BGREWRITEAOF.summary: Asynchronously rewrites the append-only file to disk.

BGSAVE.summary: Asynchronously saves the database(s) to disk.

BITCOUNT.summary: Counts the number of set bits (population counting) in a string.

BITFIELD.summary: Performs arbitrary bitfield integer operations on strings.

BITFIELD_RO.summary: Performs arbitrary read-only bitfield integer operations on strings.

BITOP.summary: Performs bitwise operations on multiple strings, and stores the result.

BITPOS.summary: Finds the first set (1) or clear (0) bit in a string.

BLMOVE.summary: Pops an element from a list, pushes it to another list and returns it. Blocks until an element is available otherwise. Deletes the list if the last element was moved.

BLMPOP.summary: Pops the first element from one of multiple lists. Blocks until an element is available otherwise. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.

BLPOP.summary: Removes and returns the first element in a list. Blocks until an element is available otherwise. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.

BRPOP.summary: Removes and returns the last element in a list. Blocks until an element is available otherwise. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.

BRPOPLPUSH.summary: Pops an element from a list, pushes it to another list and returns it. Block until an element is available otherwise. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.

BZMPOP.summary: Removes and returns a member by score from one or more sorted sets. Blocks until a member is available otherwise. Deletes the sorted set if the last element was popped.

BZPOPMAX.summary: Removes and returns the member with the highest score from one or more sorted sets. Blocks until a member available otherwise.  Deletes the sorted set if the last element was popped.

BZPOPMIN.summary: Removes and returns the member with the lowest score from one or more sorted sets. Blocks until a member is available otherwise. Deletes the sorted set if the last element was popped.

CLIENT.summary: A container for client connection commands.

COMMAND.summary: Returns detailed information about all commands.

CONFIG.summary: A container for server configuration commands.

COPY.summary: Copies the value of a key to a new key.

DBSIZE.summary: Returns the number of keys in the database.

DECR.summary: Decrements the integer value of a key by one. Uses 0 as initial value if the key doesn't exist.

DECRBY.summary: Decrements a number from the integer value of a key. Uses 0 as initial value if the key doesn't exist.

DUMP.summary: Returns a serialized representation of the value stored at a key.

EVALSHA.summary: Executes a server-side Lua script by SHA1 digest.

EVALSHA_RO.summary: Executes a read-only server-side Lua script by SHA1 digest.

EVAL_RO.summary: Executes a read-only server-side Lua script.

EXEC.summary: Executes all commands in a transaction.

EXISTS.summary: Determines whether one or more keys exist.

EXPIRE.summary: Sets the expiration time of a key in seconds.

EXPIREAT.summary: Sets the expiration time of a key to a Unix timestamp.

EXPIRETIME.summary: Returns the expiration time of a key as a Unix timestamp.

FAILOVER.summary: Starts a coordinated failover from a server to one of its replicas.

FLUSHALL.summary: Removes all keys from all databases.

FLUSHDB.summary: Remove all keys from the current database.

GEOADD.summary: Adds one or more members to a geospatial index. The key is created if it doesn't exist.

GEODIST.summary: Returns the distance between two members of a geospatial index.

GEOHASH.summary: Returns members from a geospatial index as geohash strings.

GEOPOS.summary: Returns the longitude and latitude of members from a geospatial index.

GEORADIUS.summary: Queries a geospatial index for members within a distance from a coordinate, optionally stores the result.

GEORADIUSBYMEMBER.summary: Queries a geospatial index for members within a distance from a member, optionally stores the result.

GEORADIUSBYMEMBER_RO.summary: Returns members from a geospatial index that are within a distance from a member.

GEORADIUS_RO.summary: Returns members from a geospatial index that are within a distance from a coordinate.

GEOSEARCH.summary: Queries a geospatial index for members inside an area of a box or a circle.

GEOSEARCHSTORE.summary: Queries a geospatial index for members inside an area of a box or a circle, optionally stores the result.

GET.summary: Returns the string value of a key.

GETBIT.summary: Returns a bit value by offset.

GETDEL.summary: Returns the string value of a key after deleting the key.

GETEX.summary: Returns the string value of a key after setting its expiration time.

GETRANGE.summary: Returns a substring of the string stored at a key.

GETSET.summary: Returns the previous string value of a key after setting it to a new value.

HDEL.summary: Deletes one or more fields and their values from a hash. Deletes the hash if no fields remain.

HEXISTS.summary: Determines whether a field exists in a hash.

HGET.summary: Returns the value of a field in a hash.

HGETALL.summary: Returns all fields and values in a hash.

HINCRBY.summary: Increments the integer value of a field in a hash by a number. Uses 0 as initial value if the field doesn't exist.

HINCRBYFLOAT.summary: Increments the floating point value of a field by a number. Uses 0 as initial value if the field doesn't exist.

HKEYS.summary: Returns all fields in a hash.

HLEN.summary: Returns the number of fields in a hash.

HMGET.summary: Returns the values of all fields in a hash.

HMSET.summary: Sets the values of multiple fields.

HRANDFIELD.summary: Returns one or more random fields from a hash.

HSCAN.summary: Iterates over fields and values of a hash.

HSET.summary: Creates or modifies the value of a field in a hash.

HSETNX.summary: Sets the value of a field in a hash only when the field doesn't exist.

HSTRLEN.summary: Returns the length of the value of a field.

HVALS.summary: Returns all values in a hash.

INCR.summary: Increments the integer value of a key by one. Uses 0 as initial value if the key doesn't exist.

INCRBY.summary: Increments the integer value of a key by a number. Uses 0 as initial value if the key doesn't exist.

INCRBYFLOAT.summary: Increment the floating point value of a key by a number. Uses 0 as initial value if the key doesn't exist.

INFO.summary: Returns information and statistics about the server.

KEYS.summary: Returns all key names that match a pattern.

LASTSAVE.summary: Returns the Unix timestamp of the last successful save to disk.

LATENCY.summary: A container for latency diagnostics commands.

LINDEX.summary: Returns an element from a list by its index.

LINSERT.summary: Inserts an element before or after another element in a list.

LLEN.summary: Returns the length of a list.

LMOVE.summary: Returns an element after popping it from one list and pushing it to another. Deletes the list if the last element was moved.

LMPOP.summary: Returns multiple elements from a list after removing them. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.

LOLWUT.summary: Displays computer art and the server version

LPOP.summary: Returns the first elements in a list after removing it. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.

LPOS.summary: Returns the index of matching elements in a list.

LPUSH.summary: Prepends one or more elements to a list. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

LPUSHX.summary: Prepends one or more elements to a list only when the list exists.

LRANGE.summary: Returns a range of elements from a list.

LREM.summary: Removes elements from a list. Deletes the list if the last element was removed.

LSET.summary: Sets the value of an element in a list by its index.

LTRIM.summary: Removes elements from both ends a list. Deletes the list if all elements were trimmed.

MEMORY.summary: A container for memory diagnostics commands.

MGET.summary: Atomically returns the string values of one or more keys.

MIGRATE.summary: Atomically transfers a key from one instance to another.

MONITOR.summary: Listens for all requests received by the server in real-time.

MOVE.summary: Moves a key to another database.

MSET.summary: Atomically creates or modifies the string values of one or more keys.

MSETNX.summary: Atomically modifies the string values of one or more keys only when all keys don't exist.

OBJECT.summary: A container for object introspection commands.

PERSIST.summary: Removes the expiration time of a key.

PEXPIRE.summary: Sets the expiration time of a key in milliseconds.

PEXPIREAT.summary: Sets the expiration time of a key to a Unix milliseconds timestamp.

PEXPIRETIME.summary: Returns the expiration time of a key as a Unix milliseconds timestamp.

PFADD.summary: Adds elements to a HyperLogLog key. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

PFCOUNT.summary: Returns the approximated cardinality of the set(s) observed by the HyperLogLog key(s).

PFDEBUG.summary: Internal commands for debugging HyperLogLog values.

PFMERGE.summary: Merges one or more HyperLogLog values into a single key.

PFSELFTEST.summary: An internal command for testing HyperLogLog values.

PSETEX.summary: Sets both string value and expiration time in milliseconds of a key. The key is created if it doesn't exist.

PSUBSCRIBE.summary: Listens for messages published to channels that match one or more patterns.

PSYNC.summary: An internal command used in replication.

PTTL.summary: Returns the expiration time in milliseconds of a key.

PUBLISH.summary: Posts a message to a channel.

PUNSUBSCRIBE.summary: Stops listening to messages published to channels that match one or more patterns.

RANDOMKEY.summary: Returns a random key name from the database.

READONLY.summary: Enables read-only queries for a connection to a Valkey replica node.

READWRITE.summary: Enables read-write queries for a connection to a Valkey replica node.

RENAME.summary: Renames a key and overwrites the destination.

RENAMENX.summary: Renames a key only when the target key name doesn't exist.

REPLCONF.summary: An internal command for configuring the replication stream.

REPLICAOF.summary: Configures a server as replica of another, or promotes it to a primary.

RESTORE.summary: Creates a key from the serialized representation of a value.

RESTORE-ASKING.summary: An internal command for migrating keys in a cluster.

RPOP.summary: Returns and removes the last elements of a list. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.

RPOPLPUSH.summary: Returns the last element of a list after removing and pushing it to another list. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.

RPUSH.summary: Appends one or more elements to a list. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

RPUSHX.summary: Appends an element to a list only when the list exists.

SADD.summary: Adds one or more members to a set. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

SAVE.summary: Synchronously saves the database(s) to disk.

SCAN.summary: Iterates over the key names in the database.

SCARD.summary: Returns the number of members in a set.

SCRIPT.summary: A container for Lua scripts management commands.

SDIFF.summary: Returns the difference of multiple sets.

SDIFFSTORE.summary: Stores the difference of multiple sets in a key.

SET.summary: Sets the string value of a key, ignoring its type. The key is created if it doesn't exist.

SETBIT.summary: Sets or clears the bit at offset of the string value. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

SETEX.summary: Sets the string value and expiration time of a key. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

SETNX.summary: Set the string value of a key only when the key doesn't exist.

SETRANGE.summary: Overwrites a part of a string value with another by an offset. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

SHUTDOWN.summary: Synchronously saves the database(s) to disk and shuts down the server.

SINTER.summary: Returns the intersect of multiple sets.

SINTERCARD.summary: Returns the number of members of the intersect of multiple sets.

SINTERSTORE.summary: Stores the intersect of multiple sets in a key.

SISMEMBER.summary: Determines whether a member belongs to a set.

SLAVEOF.summary: Sets a server as a replica of another, or promotes it to being a primary.

SLOWLOG.summary: A container for slow log commands.

SMEMBERS.summary: Returns all members of a set.

SMISMEMBER.summary: Determines whether multiple members belong to a set.

SMOVE.summary: Moves a member from one set to another.

SORT.summary: Sorts the elements in a list, a set, or a sorted set, optionally storing the result.

SORT_RO.summary: Returns the sorted elements of a list, a set, or a sorted set.

SPOP.summary: Returns one or more random members from a set after removing them. Deletes the set if the last member was popped.

SPUBLISH.summary: Post a message to a shard channel

SRANDMEMBER.summary: Get one or multiple random members from a set

SREM.summary: Removes one or more members from a set. Deletes the set if the last member was removed.

SSCAN.summary: Iterates over members of a set.

SSUBSCRIBE.summary: Listens for messages published to shard channels.

STRLEN.summary: Returns the length of a string value.

SUBSCRIBE.summary: Listens for messages published to channels.

SUBSTR.summary: Returns a substring from a string value.

SUNION.summary: Returns the union of multiple sets.

SUNIONSTORE.summary: Stores the union of multiple sets in a key.

SUNSUBSCRIBE.summary: Stops listening to messages posted to shard channels.

SYNC.summary: An internal command used in replication.

TOUCH.summary: Returns the number of existing keys out of those specified after updating the time they were last accessed.

TTL.summary: Returns the expiration time in seconds of a key.

TYPE.summary: Determines the type of value stored at a key.

UNLINK.summary: Asynchronously deletes one or more keys.

UNSUBSCRIBE.summary: Stops listening to messages posted to channels.

UNWATCH.summary: Forgets about watched keys of a transaction.

WAIT.summary: Blocks until the asynchronous replication of all preceding write commands sent by the connection is completed.

WAITAOF.summary: Blocks until all of the preceding write commands sent by the connection are written to the append-only file of the primary and/or replicas.

WATCH.summary: Monitors changes to keys to determine the execution of a transaction.

XACK.summary: Returns the number of messages that were successfully acknowledged by the consumer group member of a stream.

XADD.summary: Appends a new message to a stream. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

XAUTOCLAIM.summary: Changes, or acquires, ownership of messages in a consumer group, as if the messages were delivered to as consumer group member.

XCLAIM.summary: Changes, or acquires, ownership of a message in a consumer group, as if the message was delivered a consumer group member.

XDEL.summary: Returns the number of messages after removing them from a stream.

XGROUP.summary: A container for consumer groups commands.

XINFO.summary: A container for stream introspection commands.

XLEN.summary: Return the number of messages in a stream.

XPENDING.summary: Returns the information and entries from a stream consumer group's pending entries list.

XRANGE.summary: Returns the messages from a stream within a range of IDs.

XREAD.summary: Returns messages from multiple streams with IDs greater than the ones requested. Blocks until a message is available otherwise.

XREADGROUP.summary: Returns new or historical messages from a stream for a consumer in a group. Blocks until a message is available otherwise.

XREVRANGE.summary: Returns the messages from a stream within a range of IDs in reverse order.

XSETID.summary: An internal command for replicating stream values.

XTRIM.summary: Deletes messages from the beginning of a stream.

ZADD.summary: Adds one or more members to a sorted set, or updates their scores. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

ZCARD.summary: Returns the number of members in a sorted set.

ZCOUNT.summary: Returns the count of members in a sorted set that have scores within a range.

ZDIFF.summary: Returns the difference between multiple sorted sets.

ZDIFFSTORE.summary: Stores the difference of multiple sorted sets in a key.

ZINCRBY.summary: Increments the score of a member in a sorted set.

ZINTER.summary: Returns the intersect of multiple sorted sets.

ZINTERCARD.summary: Returns the number of members of the intersect of multiple sorted sets.

ZINTERSTORE.summary: Stores the intersect of multiple sorted sets in a key.

ZLEXCOUNT.summary: Returns the number of members in a sorted set within a lexicographical range.

ZMPOP.summary: Returns the highest- or lowest-scoring members from one or more sorted sets after removing them. Deletes the sorted set if the last member was popped.

ZMSCORE.summary: Returns the score of one or more members in a sorted set.

ZPOPMAX.summary: Returns the highest-scoring members from a sorted set after removing them. Deletes the sorted set if the last member was popped.

ZPOPMIN.summary: Returns the lowest-scoring members from a sorted set after removing them. Deletes the sorted set if the last member was popped.

ZRANDMEMBER.summary: Returns one or more random members from a sorted set.

ZRANGE.summary: Returns members in a sorted set within a range of indexes.

ZRANGEBYLEX.summary: Returns members in a sorted set within a lexicographical range.

ZRANGEBYSCORE.summary: Returns members in a sorted set within a range of scores.

ZRANGESTORE.summary: Stores a range of members from sorted set in a key.

ZRANK.summary: Returns the index of a member in a sorted set ordered by ascending scores.

ZREM.summary: Removes one or more members from a sorted set. Deletes the sorted set if all members were removed.

ZREMRANGEBYLEX.summary: Removes members in a sorted set within a lexicographical range. Deletes the sorted set if all members were removed.

ZREMRANGEBYRANK.summary: Removes members in a sorted set within a range of indexes. Deletes the sorted set if all members were removed.

ZREMRANGEBYSCORE.summary: Removes members in a sorted set within a range of scores. Deletes the sorted set if all members were removed.

ZREVRANGE.summary: Returns members in a sorted set within a range of indexes in reverse order.

ZREVRANGEBYLEX.summary: Returns members in a sorted set within a lexicographical range in reverse order.

ZREVRANGEBYSCORE.summary: Returns members in a sorted set within a range of scores in reverse order.

ZREVRANK.summary: Returns the index of a member in a sorted set ordered by descending scores.

ZSCAN.summary: Iterates over members and scores of a sorted set.

ZSCORE.summary: Returns the score of a member in a sorted set.

ZUNION.summary: Returns the union of multiple sorted sets.

ZUNIONSTORE.summary: Stores the union of multiple sorted sets in a key.
